input.background: The time to failure of a component has a gamma distribution with $\alpha =3$ and $\beta=5$.
input.question: Provide the mean, variance, reliability, and coefficient of skewness for the gamma random variable.

output.answer: The mean, variance and reliability of the gamma random variable are, respectively $$ \begin{gathered} \ \text { Mean (MTTF) } \quad E(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta} \\ \ \text { Variance } \quad V(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta^2} \\ \ \text { Reliability } \quad R(t)=\int_t^{\infty} \frac{\beta^\alpha x^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-x \beta} d x \end{gathered} $$ The coefficient of skewness of this gamma distribution is given by $$ S_c=\frac{2}{\sqrt{\alpha}} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
